english_literature_missing_courses.title: Creative Writing Fundamentals
english_literature_missing_courses.description: Introduction to creative writing techniques across multiple genres including fiction, poetry, and creative nonfiction. Students develop their creative voice while learning fundamental craft elements and participating in workshop-style critiques.
learning_objectives: Develop proficiency in multiple creative writing genres
Apply fundamental craft elements to original work
Participate effectively in writing workshops and peer critique
Understand the revision process and develop editing skills
Explore personal voice and creative expression
covered_materials: Elements of fiction (plot, character, setting, point of view)
Reading as a writer - analyzing published works

english_literature_missing_courses.title: Advanced Composition
english_literature_missing_courses.description: Advanced writing course focusing on argumentation, research, and rhetorical strategies for academic and professional contexts. Students develop sophisticated writing skills while learning to adapt their writing for different audiences and purposes.
learning_objectives: Construct complex arguments with sophisticated evidence
Adapt writing style for different audiences and purposes
Conduct advanced research and integrate sources effectively
Apply rhetorical strategies for persuasive writing
Develop advanced revision and editing skills
covered_materials: Citation and documentation in multiple styles

english_literature_missing_courses.title: British Literature I
english_literature_missing_courses.description: Survey of British literature from the medieval period through the 18th century including major authors, movements, and historical contexts. Students analyze representative works while understanding literary development within historical frameworks.
learning_objectives: Understand major periods and movements in early British literature
Analyze representative works within historical and cultural contexts
Recognize evolution of literary forms and conventions
Apply critical reading and analytical skills
Connect literary works to broader cultural and historical themes
covered_materials: Metaphysical and cavalier poetry (Donne, Herbert, Herrick)

english_literature_missing_courses.title: British Literature II
english_literature_missing_courses.description: Survey of British literature from the Romantic period through the contemporary era including major authors, movements, and cultural developments. Students explore the evolution of British literary expression in modern contexts.
learning_objectives: Understand major movements in modern British literature
Analyze works within social and political contexts
Trace evolution of literary forms and themes
Apply various critical approaches to literary analysis
Examine relationships between literature and cultural change
covered_materials: Romantic poetry and prose (Wordsworth, Coleridge, Byron, Shelley, Keats)

english_literature_missing_courses.title: American Literature I
english_literature_missing_courses.description: Survey of American literature from colonial times through the Civil War including major authors, themes, and cultural contexts. Students examine the development of distinctly American literary traditions and voices.
learning_objectives: Understand the development of American literary traditions
Analyze works within American historical and cultural contexts
Examine themes of identity, democracy, and cultural diversity
Apply critical reading skills to diverse American voices
Connect literature to broader American cultural movements
covered_materials: Colonial and Puritan literature (Bradford, Winthrop, Edwards)
Revolutionary and early national literature (Franklin, Jefferson, Irving)
Native American oral traditions and early written works

english_literature_missing_courses.title: American Literature II
english_literature_missing_courses.description: Survey of American literature from the Civil War to the present including major authors, movements, and cultural developments. Students explore modern and contemporary American literary expression and its relationship to social change.
learning_objectives: Understand major movements in modern American literature
Analyze literature within contexts of social and cultural change
Examine diverse voices and perspectives in American writing
Apply contemporary critical approaches
Evaluate literature's role in addressing social issues
covered_materials: Realism and naturalism (Twain, James, Crane, Dreiser)
Multicultural voices (African American, Latino/a, Asian American, Native American)

english_literature_missing_courses.title: World Literature
english_literature_missing_courses.description: Survey of major works from global literary traditions including European, Asian, African, and Latin American literature. Students explore diverse cultural perspectives while examining universal themes and cross-cultural literary influences.
learning_objectives: Understand major works from diverse world literary traditions
Analyze literature within cultural and historical contexts
Compare themes and techniques across cultures
Examine cross-cultural literary influences and exchanges
Develop global perspectives on literary expression
covered_materials: Asian literature (Chinese, Japanese, Indian classics and modern works)
Latin American literature (magical realism, political themes)

english_literature_missing_courses.title: Contemporary Literature
english_literature_missing_courses.description: Study of literature published since 1960 including fiction, poetry, and drama from diverse voices and perspectives. Students examine current literary trends while analyzing works that address contemporary social and cultural issues.
learning_objectives: Analyze major trends and movements in contemporary literature
Examine diverse voices and perspectives in current writing
Understand literature's response to contemporary issues
Apply current critical approaches to recent works
Evaluate the evolution of literary forms and techniques
covered_materials: Graphic novels and comics as literature

english_literature_missing_courses.title: Poetry and Poetics
english_literature_missing_courses.description: Comprehensive study of poetry across periods and cultures including form, technique, and theoretical approaches to understanding poetic expression. Students analyze diverse poetic traditions while developing skills in poetic interpretation.
learning_objectives: Understand major poetic forms and techniques
Analyze poetry from various periods and cultures
Apply prosodic analysis and scansion skills
Examine theories of poetic expression and meaning
Develop sophisticated skills in poetic interpretation
covered_materials: Poetic forms (sonnet, villanelle, epic, lyric, dramatic)

english_literature_missing_courses.title: Shakespeare Studies
english_literature_missing_courses.description: Intensive study of Shakespeare's major plays and poetry including textual analysis, historical context, and performance history. Students explore Shakespeare's artistic development while examining his enduring influence on literature and culture.
learning_objectives: Analyze major Shakespearean plays and poetry
Understand Elizabethan and Jacobean historical contexts
Examine Shakespeare's artistic development and techniques
Evaluate performance history and interpretations
Assess Shakespeare's influence on subsequent literature and culture
covered_materials: Major tragedies (Hamlet, Othello, King Lear, Macbeth)
